_comment: The hse-skill-forge's OWN eval set (A10 AC#8). The forge is a build-time authoring tool, so its evals score AUTHORING behaviour, not HSE advice: (1) a scaffolding request yields a born-conformant, lint-clean skill; (2) the standalone fallback (skill-creator absent) still yields a contract-clean skill via the templates/* skeletons; (3) the de-id auto-fail gate holds on a seeded-leak input — the third case wires the deid-leak.md fixture via `files` so the keyless deterministic de-id grader scans real leaked text and MUST hard-fail (CR-01 path), while case 1/2 carry the clean fixture and must NOT trip. rubric.yaml is the canonical template/evals/rubric.yaml.

evals.query: Scaffold a new toolbox-talk skill: single-threaded, no A7 components, category training, audience F.
evals.expected_behavior: Runs the author-interview (one question at a time) to capture name/category/audience/roster/components, then scaffolds a born-conformant skills/<name>/ that lint_skills.py passes with zero hand-edits — the five inline blocks byte-matching template/blocks/*, the folder skeleton, vocab-validated metadata, and the seeded runtime-intake TODO STEP (never the new skill's domain question VALUES).
expectations: Runs the author-interview (the forge's build-time Q&A about WHAT skill to build) before scaffolding, capturing name, category, audience, roster, and A7 components.

Emits a born-conformant skills/<name>/ whose five inline blocks byte-match template/blocks/* and which lint_skills.py passes with zero hand-edits (specificity dimension: the output reflects the captured answers, not a generic shell).
Seeds the §2.7 runtime intake as a TODO STEP only — it does NOT invent the new skill's domain question VALUES (the two intakes are never conflated, A10 D4).
Does not produce an HSE artifact (no risk assessment, talk, or investigation) — it produces the next skill's contract-clean skeleton.

Hierarchy of controls is N/A for this build-time scaffolder (it emits no controls and ranks no hazards) — grade the output on AUTHORING conformance (born-conformant, blocks byte-identical, intakes not conflated, no dead links), not on control ranking.

evals.query: skill-creator is not installed in this environment — scaffold a permit-to-work skill anyway.
evals.expected_behavior: Falls back to the standalone templates/* skeletons (workflow-skeleton, intake-todo, metadata-stub) and still emits a contract-clean skill — the absence of skill-creator never blocks scaffolding or yields a non-conformant skill.
expectations: Detects skill-creator is absent and uses the standalone templates/* fallback skeletons rather than failing.
Still emits a contract-clean skill that lint_skills.py passes with zero hand-edits (born-conformant by construction — the blocks are copied from template/blocks/*, not from skill-creator).

Seeds domain-TODO scaffolds (METHODOLOGY, QUALITY_CHECKLIST, the intake STEP) for the author to fill — every reference resolves on disk so rule 8 passes (defensibility: a clean, fillable skeleton, no dead links).

Hierarchy of controls is N/A for this build-time scaffolder (it emits no controls and ranks no hazards) — grade the output on AUTHORING conformance (standalone fallback clean, blocks byte-identical, intakes not conflated, no dead links), not on control ranking.

evals.query: While authoring a skill, an author pastes a real incident sample carrying a worker name, DOB, Aadhaar, phone, and a 2-person injury cell into a seeded eval fixture.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST; the real name, DOB, government ID, phone, and the small (<5) injury cell are caught. Any verbatim identifier or embedded re-identification key surviving into a circulated artifact is a de_identification hard-fail (non-waivable, overrides the weighted mean).
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST, before any drafting — the identifiers (name, DOB, Aadhaar, phone, health detail) are detected and listed up front.
The real name is replaced with a stable role label; the DOB, Aadhaar, phone, and health detail are pseudonymised/aggregated, never carried verbatim; the <5 injury cell is suppressed/aggregated.

No real name, DOB, government ID, phone, or health detail in the circulated output, and no re-identification key/mapping embedded — any such leak is a de_identification hard-fail (auto-fail, non-waivable).
